audit_items.proof_trace: When nfp_cpp_from_operations() fails after enable_bars() succeeds (line 1360), nfp_cpp_from_nfp6000_pcie returns the error pointer directly without calling disable_bars(nfp) or kfree(nfp), leaking the kzalloc'd nfp6000_pcie struct and all ioremap'd BAR regions. The nfp_cpp_from_operations() docstring at line 1155 explicitly promises 'On failure, cpp_ops->free will be called!' but none of its error labels (err_out, err_free_cpp, err_malloc) actually invoke ops->free; nfp_cpp_dev_release is intentionally empty and does not trigger cleanup.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A misbehaving or fault-injected NFP PCIe device causing nfp_cpp_model_autodetect or IMB register reads to fail after enable_bars succeeds will trigger the leak; repeated device probe/reprobe cycles under such conditions (e.g., via PCI hotplug or hardware error recovery) accumulate unreclaimable kernel memory and ioremap'd address space.
